consistency.class_match: The feature belongs to the 'monk' class, which is consistent with typical D&D 5e class progression.
consistency.level_placement: Level 7 is the standard level for the Evasion feature in the Monk class.
consistency.mechanics_consistency: The mechanical description aligns perfectly with the established D&D 5e rules for the Monk's Evasion feature.

quality.clarity: The description is clear and concise, explaining the benefit during Dexterity saving throws against area effects.
quality.completeness: It fully describes the outcome for both success (no damage) and failure (half damage) on the save.
quality.formatting: The use of markdown links ('[_fireball_](#fireball)') suggests source material integration, which is good practice, though the link itself isn't validated here.

mechanics.core_effect: Transforms a standard Dexterity save (half damage on success, full on failure) into a superior save (no damage on success, half on failure).
mechanics.synergy: Highly synergistic with the Monk's high Dexterity score and proficiency bonus, which naturally makes Dexterity saves better.
mechanics.power_level: A very strong defensive feature, dramatically increasing survivability against major area-of-effect spells and abilities.

analysis.recommendations: No changes required; the data is accurate and well-formed.
